remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: Progressive structural damage (sacroiliac fusion, syndesmophytes) in majority without treatment; spontaneous radiographic remission essentially zero; ~10% achieve clinical remission without anti-inflammatory therapy
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: TNF inhibitors (infliximab, adalimumab): ASAS20 response in 60-70%, clinical remission (ASDAS-ID) in 20-40%; IL-17 inhibitors (secukinumab, ixekizumab): equivalent or superior efficacy; remission rates with early biologics in non-radiographic axSpA may reach 40-50%
remission.gap_size: Large — most patients spend years on NSAIDs before biologic assessment; diagnostic delay averages 7-10 years
remission.barrier_detail: Diagnostic delay of 7-10 years from symptom onset is the most significant modifiable factor; MRI-based diagnosis of nr-axSpA (non-radiographic) can enable earlier biologic initiation before structural damage; HLA-B27 testing remains underused in primary care assessment of inflammatory back pain
remission.last_soc_change: 2023 — bimekizumab (dual IL-17A/F) approved for AS; 2019 secukinumab and ixekizumab established for AS/nr-axSpA; tofacitinib and upadacitinib (JAK inhibitors) added; TNF inhibitors as backbone since 2000
remission.outdated_treatments: Long-term high-dose NSAID use without biologic assessment after 2+ NSAID failures; sulfasalazine/methotrexate for axial disease (ineffective for spinal involvement — standard for peripheral arthritis only, but sometimes prescribed for axial symptoms); glucocorticoid injections as sole intervention for sacroiliac joint involvement without systemic therapy
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Exercise (TNF inhibitor trial adjunct — exercise significantly increases response rates); hydrotherapy; standing desks and postural interventions; smoking cessation (accelerates radiographic progression); vitamin D optimisation
remission.frontier_signal: Bimekizumab (dual IL-17A/F, superior radiographic inhibition vs IL-17A alone); JAK inhibitors (upadacitinib 2022 AS approval, filgotinib Phase 3); IL-23 inhibitors for peripheral/enthesitis manifestations; gut microbiome modulation (strong gut-axis link in axSpA)
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.
